findings.one_line: The compactness proof does not close the lower tube under weak-* limits: from `G_n(y) < u` for every `y < c_-(u)` it only gets `G(y) <= u`, which is not enough to conclude `Q_a^w(u|x) >= c_-(u)`.

The displayed sharp coupled endpoints and equality criterion rely on `lem:joint-feasible-extrema-attained`; until the jointly feasible class is actually shown compact/closed, the optimizer `Qcpl_a_plus`/`Qcpl_a_minus` is not fully discharged.

The lemma names the active-face EIF correction by citing Milgrom-Segal and Firpo, but it never derives the pathwise derivative of the optimizing latent CDF or proves that `phi_tube_plus` is a canonical gradient from the stated DAG.

Consistency is claimed from nuisance-rate assumptions alone, but the proof silently needs uniform convergence of the sample criterion and stability/measurability of the estimated jointly feasible class, none of which is supplied by the declared dependencies.
